#REDIRECT[[й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ]]

== жҰӮиҰҒ == зӨҫдјқгҒ§гҒҜ[[зҷҪйіі]]2е№ҙпјҲ673е№ҙпјүгҒ«еүөе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеҪ“еҲқгҒҜгҖҢзҘқпјҲгҒ»гҒҶгӮҠпјүзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒ—гҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ[[иІһиҰі]]е№ҙй–“гҒ«[[еҚғжӣІе·қ]]гҒ®жҙӘж°ҙгӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҒҰгҖҒеҹҙз§‘[[йғЎеҸё]]еӨ§й ҳгҒ®[[йҮ‘еҲәйғЁж°Ҹ|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·]]гҒҢгҖҒ[[жҜ”еҸЎеұұ]][[ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ]]гӮҲгӮҠеұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫгӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҰзӣёж®ҝгҒ«еҗҲзҘҖгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮиҝ‘дё–гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜ[[зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ]]гҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҖҒзӨҫй ҳ300зҹігҒ®д»–гҖҒзҘӯе…ёж–ҷгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰе»©зұі5ж–—е…Ҙ10дҝөгҒӘгҒ©гҒҢеҜ„йҖІгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеәҰйҮҚгҒӘгӮӢжҙӘж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ[[еҳүж°ё]]4е№ҙпјҲ1851е№ҙпјүгҒ«[[з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺ]]еҜҢжҳҢгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҶҚе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ[[еӨ©жҳҺ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ1781е№ҙпјүзҘһзҘҮз®Ўй ҳ[[еҗүз”°е®¶]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҢеұұзҺӢеӨ§жЁ©зҸҫе®®гҖҚгҒӢгӮүгҖҢй Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁж”№гӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒҜйӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫгҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ§зҘһијҝгҒҢиЎҢгҒҚжқҘгҒҷгӮӢзҘһдәӢгҒҢгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒжҳҺжІ»5е№ҙпјҲ1872е№ҙпјүгҒ«е»ғжӯўгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ
зӨҫең°гҒҜжқұиҘҝзҙ„27й–“гҖҒеҚ—еҢ—зҙ„34й–“гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒӢгӮүж°ҙзҘһгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰдҝЎд»°гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒзҹўд»Јз”Ёж°ҙе °гҒ®е®Ҳиӯ·зҘһгҒ§гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ

== жҰӮиҰҒ == зӨҫдјқгҒ§гҒҜ[[зҷҪйіі]]2е№ҙпјҲ673е№ҙпјүгҒ«еүөе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеҪ“еҲқгҒҜ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®гҖҢзҘқпјҲгҒ»гҒҶгӮҠпјүзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒ—гҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ[[иІһиҰі]]е№ҙй–“гҒ«[[еҚғжӣІе·қ]]гҒ®жҙӘж°ҙгӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҒҰгҖҒ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[еӨ§й ҳ]]гҒ®йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·гҒҢгҖҒ[[жҜ”еҸЎеұұ]][[ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ]]гӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҰзӣёж®ҝгҒ«еҗҲзҘҖгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮиҝ‘дё–гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜ[[зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ]]гҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҖҒзӨҫй ҳ300зҹігҒ®д»–гҖҒзҘӯе…ёж–ҷгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰе»©зұі5ж–—е…Ҙ10дҝөгҒӘгҒ©гҒҢеҜ„йҖІгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеәҰйҮҚгҒӘгӮӢжҙӘж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ[[еҳүж°ё]]4е№ҙпјҲ1851е№ҙпјүгҒ«[[з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺ]]еҜҢжҳҢгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҶҚе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ[[еӨ©жҳҺ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ1781е№ҙпјүзҘһзҘҮз®Ўй ҳ[[еҗүз”°е®¶]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҢеұұзҺӢеӨ§жЁ©зҸҫе®®гҖҚгҒӢгӮүгҖҢй Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁж”№гӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒҜйӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫгҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ§зҘһијҝгҒҢиЎҢгҒҚжқҘгҒҷгӮӢзҘһдәӢгҒҢгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒжҳҺжІ»5е№ҙпјҲ1872е№ҙпјүгҒ«е»ғжӯўгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ

== ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ == *'''зҘқзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒҜгҒөгӮҠгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү *:зҘӯзҘһгҒҜ[[жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|е»әдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[з§‘йҮҺеӣҪйҖ |з§‘йҮҺеӣҪгҒ®еӣҪйҖ ]]пјүгҖҒ[[е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[и«ҸиЁӘеӨ§зӨҫ|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ]]зҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҖҒеҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘ҪпјҲи«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫзҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҒ®дёүжҹұгҒ§гҖҒгҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі]]гҖҸ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®зҘқзҘһзӨҫгҒ®жҜ”е®ҡи«–зӨҫгҒ®дёҖгҒӨгҖӮ *й«ҳеёӮеӨ§зҘһзӨҫ *й«ҳиҰӢзӨҫ *йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ гҒӘгҒ©
== гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ == *[[гҒ—гҒӘгҒ®йү„йҒ“з·ҡ]][[еұӢд»Јй§…]]гҒӢгӮүеҢ—гҒёзҙ„500m

revision.comment: BotгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢ: [[Special:Permalink/83490336#Maplink2гҒЁOSM Location mapгғҶгғігғ—гғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ«жёЎгҒҷеј•ж•°гҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢCoord2гғҶгғігғ—гғ¬гғјгғҲгӮ’CoordгғҶгғігғ—гғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ«зҪ®жҸӣгҒҷгӮӢ|BotдҪңжҘӯдҫқй ј]] {{[[Template:Maplink2|Maplink2]]}}гҒЁ{{[[Template:OSM Location map|OSM Location map]]}}гҒ«жёЎгҒҷеә§жЁҷгӮ’{{[[Template:Coord2|Coord2]]}}гҒӢгӮү{{[[Template:Coord|Coord]]}}гҒ«еӨүжӣҙ
revision.text:
{{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ |з”»еғҸ = {{з”»еғҸеӢҹйӣҶдёӯ|cat=еҚғжӣІеёӮ}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[й•·йҮҺзңҢ]][[еҚғжӣІеёӮ]]еӨ§еӯ—еұӢд»Ј1858 |дҪҚзҪ®= {{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |зҘӯзҘһ = еӨ§еӣҪдё»еӨ§зҘһпјҲдё»зҘӯзҘһпјү |зӨҫж ј = [[еӣҪеҸІиҰӢеңЁзӨҫ]] |еүөе»ә = |жң¬ж®ҝ = |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жңӯжүҖзӯү = |дҫӢзҘӯ = 4жңҲ15ж—ҘгҖҖ |зҘһдәӢ = }} '''й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒҷгҒҷгҒҚгҒҝгҒҡгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјүгҒҜ[[й•·йҮҺзңҢ]][[еҚғжӣІеёӮ]]гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢзҘһзӨҫгҖӮејҸеӨ–зӨҫй ҲгҖ…еІҗж°ҙзҘһгҒ®жҜ”е®ҡзӨҫгҒ§[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|ж—§зӨҫж ј]]гҒҜж—§йғ·зӨҫгҖӮ
== жҰӮиҰҒ == зӨҫдјқгҒ§гҒҜ[[зҷҪйіі]]2е№ҙпјҲ673е№ҙпјүгҒ«еүөе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеҪ“еҲқгҒҜ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®гҖҢзҘқпјҲгҒ»гҒҶгӮҠпјүзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒ—гҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ[[иІһиҰі]]е№ҙй–“гҒ«[[еҚғжӣІе·қ]]гҒ®жҙӘж°ҙгӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҒҰгҖҒ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[еӨ§й ҳ]]гҒ®йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·гҒҢгҖҒ[[жҜ”еҸЎеұұ]][[ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ]]гӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҰзӣёж®ҝгҒ«еҗҲзҘҖгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮиҝ‘дё–гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜ[[зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ]]гҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҖҒзӨҫй ҳ300зҹігҒ®д»–гҖҒзҘӯе…ёж–ҷгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰе»©зұі5ж–—е…Ҙ10дҝөгҒӘгҒ©гҒҢеҜ„йҖІгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеәҰйҮҚгҒӘгӮӢжҙӘж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ[[еҳүж°ё]]4е№ҙпјҲ1851е№ҙпјүгҒ«[[з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺ]]еҜҢжҳҢгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҶҚе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ[[еӨ©жҳҺ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ1781е№ҙпјүзҘһзҘҮз®Ўй ҳ[[еҗүз”°е®¶]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҢеұұзҺӢеӨ§жЁ©зҸҫе®®гҖҚгҒӢгӮүгҖҢй Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁж”№гӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒҜйӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫгҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ§зҘһијҝгҒҢиЎҢгҒҚжқҘгҒҷгӮӢзҘһдәӢгҒҢгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒжҳҺжІ»5е№ҙпјҲ1872е№ҙпјүгҒ«е»ғжӯўгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ
зӨҫең°гҒҜжқұиҘҝзҙ„27й–“гҖҒеҚ—еҢ—зҙ„34й–“гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒӢгӮү[[ж°ҙзҘһ]]гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰдҝЎд»°гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒзҹўд»Јз”Ёж°ҙе °гҒ®е®Ҳиӯ·зҘһгҒ§гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ
== зҘӯзҘһ == === дё»зҘӯзҘһ === *[[еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ]]пјҲдё»зҘӯзҘһпјү *[[еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|з”ҹйӯӮе‘Ҫ]] *[[дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ]] *[[ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|зҪ”еғҸеҘіе‘Ҫ]]
=== й…ҚзҘҖ === *[[иұҠзҺүе§«е‘Ҫ]] *[[йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ]] *[[йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ]] *[[е°‘еҪҰеҗҚе‘Ҫ]] *[[дҝқйЈҹе‘Ҫ]] *[[еӣҪеёёз«Ӣе‘Ҫ]]
== ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ == *'''зҘқзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒҜгҒөгӮҠгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү *:зҘӯзҘһгҒҜ[[жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|е»әдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[з§‘йҮҺеӣҪйҖ |з§‘йҮҺеӣҪгҒ®еӣҪйҖ ]]пјүгҖҒ[[е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[и«ҸиЁӘеӨ§зӨҫ|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ]]зҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҖҒеҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘ҪпјҲи«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫзҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҒ®дёүжҹұгҒ§гҖҒгҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі]]гҖҸ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®зҘқзҘһзӨҫгҒ®жҜ”е®ҡи«–зӨҫгҒ®дёҖгҒӨгҖӮ *й«ҳеёӮеӨ§зҘһзӨҫ *й«ҳиҰӢзӨҫ *йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ гҒӘгҒ©
== гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ == *[[гҒ—гҒӘгҒ®йү„йҒ“з·ҡ]][[еұӢд»Јй§…]]гҒӢгӮүеҢ—гҒёзҙ„500m
== еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == *гҖҺ[[и§’е·қж—Ҙжң¬ең°еҗҚеӨ§иҫһе…ё]] 20 й•·йҮҺзңҢгҖҸ [[и§’е·қжӣёеә—]]гҖҒ1990е№ҙ *[[и°·е·қеҒҘдёҖ]]з·Ё гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°- 9 зҫҺжҝғгғ»йЈӣйЁЁгғ»дҝЎжҝғгҖҸ [[зҷҪж°ҙзӨҫ]]гҖҒ2000е№ҙ
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=й•·йҮҺзңҢ}} {{зҘһйҒ“ жЁӘ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒҷгҒҷгҒҚгҒҝгҒҷгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:й•·йҮҺзңҢгҒ®зҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:еҚғжӣІеёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

{{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ |з”»еғҸ = {{з”»еғҸеӢҹйӣҶдёӯ|cat=еҚғжӣІеёӮ}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E}}}} |жүҖеңЁең° = [[й•·йҮҺзңҢ]][[еҚғжӣІеёӮ]]еӨ§еӯ—еұӢд»Ј1858 |дҪҚзҪ®= {{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |зҘӯзҘһ = еӨ§еӣҪдё»еӨ§зҘһпјҲдё»зҘӯзҘһпјү |зӨҫж ј = [[еӣҪеҸІиҰӢеңЁзӨҫ]] |еүөе»ә = |жң¬ж®ҝ = |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жңӯжүҖзӯү = |дҫӢзҘӯ = 4жңҲ15ж—ҘгҖҖ |зҘһдәӢ = }} '''й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒҷгҒҷгҒҚгҒҝгҒҡгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјүгҒҜ[[й•·йҮҺзңҢ]][[еҚғжӣІеёӮ]]гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢзҘһзӨҫгҖӮејҸеӨ–зӨҫй ҲгҖ…еІҗж°ҙзҘһгҒ®жҜ”е®ҡзӨҫгҒ§[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|ж—§зӨҫж ј]]гҒҜж—§йғ·зӨҫгҖӮ
== жҰӮиҰҒ == зӨҫдјқгҒ§гҒҜ[[зҷҪйіі]]2е№ҙпјҲ673е№ҙпјүгҒ«еүөе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеҪ“еҲқгҒҜ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®гҖҢзҘқпјҲгҒ»гҒҶгӮҠпјүзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒ—гҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ[[иІһиҰі (ж—Ҙжң¬)|иІһиҰі]]е№ҙй–“гҒ«[[еҚғжӣІе·қ]]гҒ®жҙӘж°ҙгӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҒҰгҖҒ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[еӨ§й ҳ]]гҒ®йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·гҒҢгҖҒ[[жҜ”еҸЎеұұ]][[ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ]]гӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҰзӣёж®ҝгҒ«еҗҲзҘҖгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮиҝ‘дё–гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜ[[зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ]]гҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҖҒзӨҫй ҳ300зҹігҒ®д»–гҖҒзҘӯе…ёж–ҷгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰе»©зұі5ж–—е…Ҙ10дҝөгҒӘгҒ©гҒҢеҜ„йҖІгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеәҰйҮҚгҒӘгӮӢжҙӘж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ[[еҳүж°ё]]4е№ҙпјҲ1851е№ҙпјүгҒ«[[з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺ]]еҜҢжҳҢгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҶҚе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ[[еӨ©жҳҺ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ1781е№ҙпјүзҘһзҘҮз®Ўй ҳ[[еҗүз”°е®¶]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҢеұұзҺӢеӨ§жЁ©зҸҫе®®гҖҚгҒӢгӮүгҖҢй Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁж”№гӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒҜйӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫгҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ§зҘһијҝгҒҢиЎҢгҒҚжқҘгҒҷгӮӢзҘһдәӢгҒҢгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒжҳҺжІ»5е№ҙпјҲ1872е№ҙпјүгҒ«е»ғжӯўгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ
зӨҫең°гҒҜжқұиҘҝзҙ„27й–“гҖҒеҚ—еҢ—зҙ„34й–“гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒӢгӮү[[ж°ҙзҘһ]]гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰдҝЎд»°гҒ•гӮҢгҖҒзҹўд»Јз”Ёж°ҙе °гҒ®е®Ҳиӯ·зҘһгҒ§гҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ

== ж‘Ӯжң«зӨҫ == *'''зҘқзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒҜгҒөгӮҠгҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјү *:зҘӯзҘһгҒҜ[[жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|е»әдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[з§‘йҮҺеӣҪйҖ |з§‘йҮҺеӣҪгҒ®еӣҪйҖ ]]пјүгҖҒ[[е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ]]пјҲ[[и«ҸиЁӘеӨ§зӨҫ|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ]]зҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҖҒеҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘ҪпјҲи«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫзҘӯзҘһгҒ®еҸӨеҗҚпјүгҒ®дёүжҹұгҒ§гҖҒгҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі]]гҖҸ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®зҘқзҘһзӨҫгҒ®жҜ”е®ҡи«–зӨҫгҒ®дёҖгҒӨгҖӮ *й«ҳеёӮеӨ§зҘһзӨҫ *й«ҳиҰӢзӨҫ *йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ гҒӘгҒ©
== гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ == *[[гҒ—гҒӘгҒ®йү„йҒ“з·ҡ]][[еұӢд»Јй§…]]гҒӢгӮүеҢ—гҒёзҙ„500m
== еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == *гҖҺ[[и§’е·қж—Ҙжң¬ең°еҗҚеӨ§иҫһе…ё]] 20 й•·йҮҺзңҢгҖҸ [[и§’е·қжӣёеә—]]гҖҒ1990е№ҙ *[[и°·е·қеҒҘдёҖ]]з·Ё гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°- 9 зҫҺжҝғгғ»йЈӣйЁЁгғ»дҝЎжҝғгҖҸ [[зҷҪж°ҙзӨҫ]]гҖҒ2000е№ҙ

== жҰӮиҰҒ == зӨҫдјқгҒ§гҒҜ[[зҷҪйіі]]2е№ҙпјҲ673е№ҙпјүгҒ«еүөе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеҪ“еҲқгҒҜ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®гҖҢзҘқпјҲгҒ»гҒҶгӮҠпјүзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒ—гҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ[[иІһиҰі (ж—Ҙжң¬)|иІһиҰі]]е№ҙй–“гҒ«[[еҚғжӣІе·қ]]гҒ®жҙӘж°ҙгӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҒҰгҖҒ[[еҹҙз§‘йғЎ]][[еӨ§й ҳ]]гҒ®[[йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·]]гҒҢгҖҒ[[жҜ”еҸЎеұұ]][[ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ]]гӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҰзӣёж®ҝгҒ«еҗҲзҘҖгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮиҝ‘дё–гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰгҒҜ[[зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ]]гҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮ’еҸ—гҒ‘гҖҒзӨҫй ҳ300зҹігҒ®д»–гҖҒзҘӯе…ёж–ҷгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰе»©зұі5ж–—е…Ҙ10дҝөгҒӘгҒ©гҒҢеҜ„йҖІгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеәҰйҮҚгҒӘгӮӢжҙӘж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҒ[[еҳүж°ё]]4е№ҙпјҲ1851е№ҙпјүгҒ«[[з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺ]]еҜҢжҳҢгҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰеҶҚе»әгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ[[еӨ©жҳҺ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ1781е№ҙпјүзҘһзҘҮз®Ўй ҳ[[еҗүз”°е®¶]]гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠгҖҢеұұзҺӢеӨ§жЁ©зҸҫе®®гҖҚгҒӢгӮүгҖҢй Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁж”№гӮҒгӮүгӮҢгҒҹгҖӮеҸӨгҒҸгҒҜйӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫгҒЁгҒ®й–“гҒ§зҘһијҝгҒҢиЎҢгҒҚжқҘгҒҷгӮӢзҘһдәӢгҒҢгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒжҳҺжІ»5е№ҙпјҲ1872е№ҙпјүгҒ«е»ғжӯўгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name=Susuki Mizu Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image={{image requested|cat=Chikuma City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}}
|location=[[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chikuma City]], ЕҢaza Yashiro 1858
|coordinates={{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity={{ill|ЕҢkuninushi no ЕҢkami|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»еӨ§зҘһ}} (Main deity)
|Shrine rank={{ill|Kokushi Genzaisha|ja|еӣҪеҸІиҰӢеңЁзӨҫ}}
|festival=April 15
}}

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ}} from {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё}} of {{ill|Hanishina District|ja|еҹҙз§‘йғЎ}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ}} during the {{ill|JЕҚgan era|ja|иІһиҰі}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei era|ja|еҳүж°ё}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|зҘһијҝ}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Water deity|ja|ж°ҙзҘһ}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Enshrined Deities ==

=== Main Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikumusubi no Mikoto|ja|з”ҹйӯӮе‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ}}

=== Subordinate Deities ===
* {{ill|Toyotamahime no Mikoto|ja|иұҠзҺүе§«е‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Hayaki Tsuhime no Mikoto|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|е°‘еҪҰеҗҚе‘Ҫ|1=Sukunabikona}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi no Mikoto|ja|дҝқйЈҹе‘Ҫ}}
* {{ill|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto|ja|еӣҪеёёз«Ӣе‘Ҫ|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* '''{{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ}}'''
** Enshrines {{ill|Take Iho Takeno Mikoto|ja|жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ}} (a {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|дҝЎжҝғеӣҪ}} local ruler buried at {{ill|Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|ja|жЈ®е°Ҷи»ҚеЎҡеҸӨеўі}}), {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ}}вҖҷs deity), and {{ill|Minakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|еҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘Ҫ}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫ}}вҖҷs deity).
* {{ill|Takachi Daijin Shrine|ja|й«ҳеёӮеӨ§зҘһзӨҫ}}
* {{ill|Takami Shrine|ja|й«ҳиҰӢзӨҫ}}
* {{ill|Sericulture Shrine|ja|йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ}}

== Access ==
* Approximately 500 meters north of {{ill|Yashiro Station|ja|еұӢд»Јй§…}} on the {{ill|Shinano Railway Line|ja|гҒ—гҒӘгҒ®йү„йҒ“з·ҡ}}

== References ==
* {{ill|Kadokawa Japan Place name Dictionary|ja|и§’е·қж—Ҙжң¬ең°еҗҚеӨ§иҫһе…ё}} Vol. 20, Nagano Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1990.
* Edited by {{ill|Ken'ichi Tanikawa|ja|и°·е·қеҒҘдёҖ}}, ''{{ill|Kami of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}}'' Vol. 9, Mino, Hida, Shinano, Hakusui-sha, 2000.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|12=draft}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=draft}} from {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ|12=draft}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=draft}} of {{ill|Hanishina District|ja|еҹҙз§‘йғЎ|12=draft}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=draft}} during the {{ill|JЕҚgan era|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ|12=draft}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei era|ja|еҳүж°ё|12=draft}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|12=draft}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ|12=draft}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|зҘһијҝ|12=draft}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Water deity|ja|ж°ҙзҘһ|12=draft}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Enshrined Deities ==

=== Main Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikumusubi no Mikoto|ja|з”ҹйӯӮе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|12=draft}}

=== Subordinate Deities ===
* {{ill|Toyotamahime no Mikoto|ja|иұҠзҺүе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Hayaki Tsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Hayaki Tsuhime no Mikoto|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|е°‘еҪҰеҗҚе‘Ҫ|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi no Mikoto|ja|дҝқйЈҹе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto|ja|еӣҪеёёз«Ӣе‘Ҫ|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|12=draft}}

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Water deity|ja|ж°ҙзҘһ|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|12=draft}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|12=draft}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=draft}} from {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|ж—ҘеҗүеӨ§зӨҫ|12=draft}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=draft}} of {{ill|Hanishina District|ja|еҹҙз§‘йғЎ|12=draft}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=draft}} during the {{ill|JЕҚgan era|ja|иІһиҰі|12=draft}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|зңҹз”°ж°Ҹ|12=draft}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei era|ja|еҳүж°ё|12=draft}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|12=draft}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|ja|еӨ©жҳҺ|12=draft}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|зҘһијҝ|12=draft}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Water deity|ja|ж°ҙзҘһ|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Enshrined Deities ==

=== Main Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikumusubi no Mikoto|ja|з”ҹйӯӮе‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|12=draft}}
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|12=draft}}

=== Subordinate Deities ===

** Enshrines {{ill|Take Iho Takeno Mikoto|ja|жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (a {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|дҝЎжҝғеӣҪ|12=draft}} local ruler buried at {{ill|Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|ja|жЈ®е°Ҷи»ҚеЎҡеҸӨеўі|12=draft}}), {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ|12=draft}}вҖҷs deity), and {{ill|Minakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|еҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘Ҫ|12=draft}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫ|12=draft}}вҖҷs deity).

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ}}.
== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions.
Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).
The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.
== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Main Deities === * {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity) * {{ill|Ikumusubi no Mikoto|ja|з”ҹйӯӮе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikumusubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҹгғ…гғҸгғҺгғЎ}}

== Auxiliary Shrines == * '''{{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Enshrines {{ill|Take Iho Takeno Mikoto|ja|жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Iho Takeno Mikoto|qq=}} (a [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] local ruler buried at {{ill|Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|ja|жЈ®е°Ҷи»ҚеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҹҙз§‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}), {{ill|Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata Tomi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҝгӮұгғҹгғҠгӮ«гӮҝ}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёҠзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}вҖҷs deity), and {{ill|Minakata Tomi no Mikoto|ja|еҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakata Tomi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an ancient name of {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|и«ҸиЁӘдёӢзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}вҖҷs deity). * {{ill|Takachi Daijin Shrine|ja|й«ҳеёӮеӨ§зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takachi Daijin Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Takami Shrine|ja|й«ҳиҰӢзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sericulture Shrine|ja|йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sericulture Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

==Merged second translation== {{зҘһзӨҫ |name = Susuki Mizu Shrine (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) |image = {{image requested|cat=Chikuma, Nagano}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]] еӨ§еӯ—еұӢд»Ј1858 |дҪҚзҪ®= {{coord|36|32|21.7|N|138|7|35.5|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»еӨ§зҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|еӣҪеҸІиҰӢеңЁзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зӨҫж ј|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |еүөе»ә = |жң¬ж®ҝ = |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жңӯжүҖзӯү = |festival = [[:en:April 15|April 15]]гҖҖ |зҘһдәӢ = }}
'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== Overview == According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|qq=}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.
In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.
Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).
The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.
== Deities ==
=== Main Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity) * {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}} (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҹгғ…гғҸгғҺгғЎ}} (Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto)
=== Additional Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|Toyotamahime|ja|иұҠзҺүе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҲгғЁгӮҝгғһгғ’гғЎ}} (Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hayaki-hiko|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘеҪҰе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaki-hiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҸгғӨгӮўгӮӯгғ„гғ’гӮігғ»гғҸгғӨгӮўгӮӯгғ„гғ’гғЎ}} (Hayaki-hiko-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hayaki-hime|ja|йҖҹз§ӢжҙҘе§«е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaki-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҸгғӨгӮўгӮӯгғ„гғ’гӮігғ»гғҸгғӨгӮўгӮӯгғ„гғ’гғЎ}} (Hayaki-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|е°‘еҪҰеҗҚе‘Ҫ|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮҜгғҠгғ“гӮігғҠ}} (Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|дҝқйЈҹе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝқйЈҹзҘһ}} (Ukemochi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|еӣҪеёёз«Ӣе‘Ҫ|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӣҪд№Ӣеёёз«ӢзҘһ}} (Kuninotokotachi-no-Mikoto)
== Auxiliary Shrines == * '''HЕҚri Shrine''' (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ) ''' Enshrines {{ill|Takehirokunioshihitohinomikoto|ja|жӯҰдә”зҷҫе»әе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehirokunioshihitohinomikoto|qq=}} (a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|з§‘йҮҺеӣҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to з§‘йҮҺеӣҪйҖ }} and associated with the {{ill|Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|ja|жЈ®е°Ҷи»ҚеЎҡеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori ShЕҚgunzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҹҙз§‘еҸӨеўізҫӨ}}), {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|е»әеҫЎеҗҚж–№еҜҢе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮҝгӮұгғҹгғҠгӮ«гӮҝ}} (ancient name of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]] upper shrine deity), and {{ill|Minakatatomi|ja|еҚ—ж–№еҲҖзҫҺе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakatatomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ancient name of the lower shrine deity).
* Takichi Shrine * Takami Shrine * Silkworm Shrine (йӨҠиҡ•зҘһзӨҫ)

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|qq=|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* '''HЕҚri Shrine''' (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ)

== Access ==
* Approximately 500 meters north of [[:en:Shinano Railway Line|Shinano Railway Line]] [[:en:Yashiro Station|Yashiro Station]].

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|qq=|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|qq=|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

== Deities ==

=== Main Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}} (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҹгғ…гғҸгғҺгғЎ}} (Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto)

=== Additional Enshrined Deities ===

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

* {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto)

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

=== Main Deities ===
