'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Enshrined Deities ==

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

== Deities ==

=== Main Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}} (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҹгғ…гғҸгғҺгғЎ}} (Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto)

=== Additional Enshrined Deities ===

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Enshrined Deities ==

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

== Deities ==

=== Main Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} (Main deity)
* {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|дәӢд»Јдё»е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дәӢд»Јдё»}} (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto)
* {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ејҘйғҪжіўиғҪеЈІзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҹгғ…гғҸгғҺгғЎ}} (Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto)

=== Additional Enshrined Deities ===
* {{ill|Toyotamahime|ja|иұҠзҺүе§«е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғҲгғЁгӮҝгғһгғ’гғЎ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}} (Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto)

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. During the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan (era)]] era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Chikuma City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of [[:en:Hanishina District, Nagano|Hanishina District]], after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the [[:en:Zhenguan|JЕҚgan era]]. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]], receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei era]] under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ([[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.
