'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

== Auxiliary Shrines ==
* '''{{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

== Deities ==

=== Main Enshrined Deities ===

* {{ill|Ikutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еӨ©жҙ»зҺүе‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}} (Ikutama-no-Mikoto)

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Chikuma City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine, with its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification as a former {{ill|GЕҚsha|ja|йғ·зӨҫ|1=GЕҚ-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GЕҚsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Overview ==

According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the second year of the [[HakuhЕҚ era]] (673 CE). Initially known as {{ill|HЕҚri Shrine|ja|зҘқзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚri Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it later enshrined {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} at the behest of {{ill|Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәиҲҺдәәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Toneri Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a {{ill|Gunji|ja|йғЎеҸё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, after flooding from the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} during the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan era|1=Zhenguan}}. In later periods, it gained the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, receiving land grants of 300 koku and festival contributions. 

Rebuilt in 1851 during the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei era|1=Kaei}} under the guidance of {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed from "SannЕҚ Daigongen Palace" to "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the {{ill|Yoshida clan|ja|еҗүз”°е®¶|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida clan|qq=}} in 1781 ({{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 1). The shrine formerly held rituals involving the exchange of {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but discontinued this in 1872 (Meiji 5).

The shrine grounds span approximately 27 ken (east to west) and 34 ken (north to south). Revered as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, it has long served as the guardian deity of the Yashiro Irrigation Weir.

'''Susuki Mizu Shrine''' (й Ҳй ҲеІҗж°ҙзҘһзӨҫ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma, Nagano|1=Chikuma, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. It is associated with the ancient {{ill|Shrines listed in Engishiki|ja|ејҸеӨ–зӨҫ|1=Shikigesha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines listed in Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Susuki Mizu Shrine and held the status of a village shrine (ж—§йғ·зӨҫ) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.

== Overview ==
According to shrine tradition, Susuki Mizu Shrine was founded in the second year of the {{ill|HakuhЕҚ period|ja|зҷҪйіі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HakuhЕҚ period|1=HakuhЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} (673). Initially, it was known as the "HЕҚri Shrine" (зҘқзҘһзӨҫ), a name derived from the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. During the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=JЕҚgan (era)|1=Zhenguan}} era, following flooding of the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|еҚғжӣІе·қ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҝЎжҝғе·қ|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}}, the regional governor of {{ill|Hanishina District, Nagano|en|Hanishina District, Nagano|WD=Q726661|lt=Hanishina District|1=Hanishina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja|йҮ‘еҲәжӯЈй•·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brought the {{ill|SannЕҚ Gongen|ja|еұұзҺӢжЁ©зҸҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/SannЕҚ Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} and enshrined it alongside the main deity.

In the Edo period, the shrine received the patronage of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}, who donated 300 koku of land and additional resources for rituals. The shrine was reconstructed by {{ill|Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja|з«Ӣе·қе’ҢеӣӣйғҺеҜҢжҳҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatekawa WashirЕҚ Tomimasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the fourth year of {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} (1851) following repeated flood damage. In the first year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} (1781), the shrine was renamed "Susuki Mizu Shrine" by the head of Shinto priests, the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|еҚңйғЁж°Ҹ|en|draft:Yoshida family|simple|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|fr|User:Immanuelle/Urabe_clan|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}}.

Previously, the shrine participated in rituals involving the transfer of portable shrines (зҘһијҝ) with {{ill|Amamiya Shrine|ja|йӣЁе®®гҒ®еқҗж—ҘеҗүзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amamiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but these were discontinued in the fifth year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1872).

The shrine site measures approximately 27 ken east-to-west and 34 ken north-to-south. It has been worshipped as a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}, serving as the guardian of the Yashiro irrigation system.
